message:
test: add edge-case conformance fixtures and boundary-condition tests

New test fixtures:
- single_issue.jsonl: minimal single-issue input for boundary tests
- all_closed.jsonl: all-closed-status corpus for zero-open assertions
- empty.jsonl: empty input (1 byte) for graceful empty-set handling
- boundary_conditions.jsonl: edge-case patterns for parser robustness
- large_graph_40.jsonl: 40-node graph for stress-testing graph metrics
  and suggest/insights scalability
- adversarial_stress.jsonl: adversarial patterns testing reopen cycles,
  label edge cases, and malformed dependency chains

New conformance tests in tests/conformance.rs:
- robot_triage_single_issue_returns_valid_output: validates triage
  produces exactly one recommendation for a single open issue
- robot_triage_all_closed_returns_zero_open: validates triage correctly
  reports zero open issues when all are closed
- robot_suggest_single_issue_no_crash: ensures suggest handles single-
  issue input without panicking
- robot_suggest_all_closed_empty_suggestions: validates suggest returns
  empty array when no open issues exist
- robot_plan_single_issue: validates plan output shape for minimal input
- robot_insights_single_issue: validates insights output with single
  open issue (zero cycles, non-empty critical path)
- robot_forecast_single_issue: validates forecast produces exactly one
  entry for single-issue input
- robot_graph_single_issue: validates graph produces exactly one node
- robot_history_single_issue: validates history output shape
- robot_insights_large_graph: validates insights handles 40-node graph
  (cycles present, metrics computed for all nodes)
- robot_suggest_large_graph: validates suggest produces suggestions
  for large dependency graph (at least 2 suggestions expected)
- History milestone null-field validation: extends existing history test
  to assert milestones never contain null-valued keys (verifying
  skip_serializing_if correctness)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
